shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: ROTARY DIAL selector — slot near BASE of dial on console. No traditional lever.
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
ZF 9HP transmission: locate the shift lock slot — ROTARY DIAL selector — slot near BASE of dial on console. No traditional lever.
Insert a thin key or dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 from dealer tool suppliers) into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
NOTE: The ZF 9HP solenoid requires more force than most vehicles — apply steady firm pressure.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
ZF 9HP STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
The ZF 9HP is very difficult to shift without 12V power — jumping the battery first is the most reliable method.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
If using mechanical slot: a dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is more reliable than a key.
vehicles.notes: ROTARY DIAL only — no shifter lever. Familiarize with the dial before attempting.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

knownIssues.title: ROTARY DIAL SELECTOR — Not a Traditional Shifter
knownIssues.detail: The 2015-2017 Chrysler 200 uses a rotary dial on the center console instead of a gear lever. Operators unfamiliar with this vehicle waste significant time looking for a shifter that does not exist. The shift lock release slot is at the BASE of the rotary dial.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Transmission Issues
knownIssues.detail: This generation used the notoriously problematic ZF 9HP transmission. Neutral override can be difficult without power. A jump pack is strongly recommended before attempting any shift lock or neutral override procedure on this vehicle.

knownIssues.title: Door Handles Require Power
knownIssues.detail: The interior door handles are electronic and require 12V power to operate. With a dead battery, occupants can be temporarily unable to exit using the door handles. Be aware of this if the vehicle is occupied.

battery.warnings: Rotary dial selector — no gear lever. ZF 9HP requires power. Jump first, then shift.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
AWD VARIANT: Use flatbed. If no flatbed, wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.

vehicles.slot: ROTARY DIAL selector — slot near BASE of dial on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: ROTARY DIAL selector — slot near BASE of dial on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

knownIssues.title: ROTARY DIAL SELECTOR — Not a Traditional Shifter
knownIssues.detail: Like the Chrysler 200, the Pacifica uses a rotary dial on the center console. No shift lever exists. The manual override slot is at the base of the dial.

knownIssues.title: Hybrid PHEV — 12V Only for Jumping
knownIssues.detail: Pacifica Hybrid has a high-voltage battery pack under the floor. ONLY use the 12V auxiliary battery under the hood for jumping. Never attempt to access or jump from the HV pack.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid: 12V auxiliary under hood only.
battery.warnings: Pacifica Hybrid PHEV: 12V auxiliary only. NEVER touch HV battery under floor.

Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: CVT (2007-2010): do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.warn: CVT models: flatbed preferred — CVT damage risk if towed with front wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. CVT: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — BATTERY IS IN THE TRUNK.
Crossfire has a trunk-mounted battery — do NOT look under the hood for jump terminals.
Open the trunk, locate the battery on the right side, connect jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Mercedes SLK-based platform. Battery is in the TRUNK — not under the hood. Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.warn: Battery is in the TRUNK — not under the hood.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Flatbed preferred due to low clearance.
towNotes: Battery is in the TRUNK — open trunk for jump access.
Low clearance — use flatbed with ramps when possible.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Trunk
knownIssues.detail: The Crossfire is Mercedes SLK-based. The 12V battery is in the TRUNK on the right side. There is no battery under the hood. Many operators waste time looking under the hood — go directly to the trunk.

battery.location: TRUNK — right side when looking in from the rear
battery.warnings: Battery is in the TRUNK — not under the hood.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on floor console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on floor console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case: shift to 2WD before road towing.
vehicles.notes: Dodge Durango-based platform. Transfer case must be in 2H/2WD for road towing.
vehicles.warn: Transfer case must be in 2H for road towing — 4WD on dry pavement damages driveline.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear. 4WD: shift TC to 2H first, then lift rear. Flatbed preferred.

tc.type: Electronic push-button or floor lever (varies by trim)
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Auto — NOT safe for road towing
4WD Hi — part-time — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH or stop completely.
Shift TC to 2WD using the console button or floor lever.
Verify all 4WD indicator lights are off before lifting rear wheels.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, shift to 2WD, then disconnect.

towNotes: Dodge Durango platform. Electronic or manual TC depending on trim.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first, shift to 2WD, THEN disconnect. If unable, FLATBED ONLY.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on console or dash-mounted column shifter
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
COLUMN SHIFTER (some trims): key to ON, press brake, move column lever to N.
FLOOR SHIFTER: Locate shift lock cap near selector base.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Early models (1991-1995) may have column shifter. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. AWD: flatbed preferred.
